synopsis: RE Henry Leissing complained that Presbytery erred when it voted on November 7, 2008 to reverse the Sealy Commission's October 23 decision and dissolve the calls of REs Linton and Pellom. The SJC sustained the complaint by reference to its decision in Case 2008-15, Issue 5, holding that Presbytery erred in appointing the Sealy Commission in the first place since the underlying August 7 congregational meeting was illegitimate.
